summary:
The effectiveness data indicates that PPV-23 provides significant benefits specifically among young infants under two due to their higher risk from unmitigated infection before completing full immunizations; thus it serves crucially here compared to older populations or those already well-vaccinated against common strains included within both vaccines' scopes." ``` To summarize Agent1’s reasoning into two sentences focusing on key clinical points:

PPV-23 is most beneficial for children younger than 2 years because they lack complete immunity during early childhood, making them highly vulnerable to serious pneumococcal diseases until fully vaccinated. This group therefore receives substantial protective value from PPV-23 beyond what routine vaccinations provide at earlier ages.
